stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 41% and 58%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $83K in good team experience and project size, $14K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $121K in good team experience and project size, $33K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $195K in good team experience and project size, $38K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.49: best option is Hybrid with EV=$114.9K. Hybrid is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [102.4, 129.1].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Hybrid is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
